text:
Per-repository health report:

| Repository | Open Pull Requests | Merged PRs Closing a Linear Issue |
|------------|-------------------|-----------------------------------|
| acme/web   | 14                | 3                                 |
| acme/mobile| 8                 | 6                                 |
| acme/api   | 7                 | 2                                 |
| acme/infra | 7                 | 4                                 |

Repository with the most open pull requests: acme/web (14 open PRs).
I'll query the pull requests data to generate the per-repository health report. Let me first check the table structure to confirm the repository column name.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    repo,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'open') AS open_prs,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL AND closes_linear != '') AS merged_prs_closing_linear
FROM github_pull_requests
GROUP BY repo
ORDER BY open_prs DESC;"}
